entries.title: The Deep Age
entries.subtitle: Ancient History
entries.body: Velnorcan civilisation began in the Deep layer of Velundra, between 300 and 700km below the surface. Early Velnorca were social hunters who developed cooperative survival strategies, shared territory and communal child rearing, and eventually primitive tool use adapted for underwater use. The lack of land or any natural barriers between groups meant that rather then fragmenting into isolated tribes, Velnorca communities remained intact and gradually merged into a single civilization, although loosely unified. Early governance was informal, built around councils of the many elders, a structure that would later evolve into the modern system.

entries.title: The Age of Ascent
entries.subtitle: Several Centuries before the Era of Light
entries.body:
As Velnorcan civilization grew so did their need for resources and space, thus they expanded into the Surface layer, such expansion came to be a great boon as the relative abundance of life in the Surface layer provided new food and materials, alongside this the discovery of an atmosphere above the water caused many to ponder the nature of their world.

Early explorers of the above water were the ones who documented the moons, Var and Mar, and later the sun, Varn. The first above-water constructions, early observation platforms, were built in this era however energy remained a problem, Velnorcan civilization was advanced but no scalable option was available beyond tidal and biological methods.
